message:
fix(swarm): propagate context.Context through auto-respawner lifecycle

The auto-respawner's processLimitEvents goroutine and Respawn method
previously used a mix of the respawner's stored context and no context
at all, making it impossible to cancel a mid-flight respawn when the
respawner is stopped.

- processLimitEvents now accepts and selects on a child context, so
  stopping the respawner immediately unblocks the event loop
- respawn() (private) accepts context and checks ctx.Err() between
  each step (kill, rotate, clear, cd, spawn, wait, inject), returning
  early with a cancellation error if the context is done
- killAgent, clearPane, cdToProject, waitForAgentReady all accept
  context and use sleepWithContext instead of raw time.Sleep
- Public Respawn() preserved for backwards compat, delegates to
  respawn(context.Background(), ...)
- Tests updated for the new signatures

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
